channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: 50+ terrorist incidents occurred in US since 2021: Report
item.description: A House committee report found the nation's security is in disrepair after more than 50 terrorist incidents since 2021
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth lawyer slams 'flawed and questionable affidavit' from ex-sister-in-law
item.description: Pete Hegseth's lawyer dissected and refuted a new affidavit from the defense secretary nominee's former sister-in-law in a letter to SASC chairman Wicker on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace says she is considering a run for South Carolina governor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina said Wednesday that she is “seriously considering” a gubernatorial run in 2026 and will make a final decision in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pardons Ross Ulbricht, Texas-born founder of dark web marketplace website
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient the U.S. government. His executive orders cover issues that range from trade, immigration and U.S. foreign aid to demographic diversity, civil rights and the hiring of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to meet with more Republicans as they hash out legislative roadmap
item.description: President Donald Trump will meet with a small contingent of the most politically endangered House Republicans as the party struggles to agree on a strategy for implementing the tax cuts that it promised voters.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to huddle with House GOP moderates at White House
item.description: A trio of moderate House Republicans are set to meet with President Trump at the White House on Wednesday, the latest huddle between the president and different GOP factions as the lower chamber prepares to pass his legislative agenda with its slim majority. Reps. Don Bacon (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson won’t ‘second-guess’ Trump pardons of violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) defended President Trump’s sweeping pardons of those who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, including numerous people who violently attacked police officers. “The president's made his decision. I don't second-guess those,” Johnson said at a press conference Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration freezes many health agency reports and posts
item.description: The Trump administration has put a freeze on many federal health agency communications with the public through at least the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe And Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Newsom uses LA fires to criticize President Trump's reversal of Biden-era climate emissions standards
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom is attempting to criticize President Donald Trump's environmental executive orders by pointing to the recent deadly fires in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump revokes Biden order allowing transgender troops in bid to rid DEI from military
item.description: President Donald Trump has revoked a Biden-era order allowing transgender people to serve in the military. There are an estimated 9,000 to 14,000 transgender troops currently serving.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What cabinet appointments for Sean Duffy, Kristi Noem, and Doug Burgum could mean for Minnesota
item.description: Three regional leaders—Doug Burgum, Kristi Noem, and Sean Duffy—are among President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet nominees. Their potential appointments could significantly influence Minnesota and neighboring states.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gracie Stockton, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump excoriates bishop as 'Radical Left hard line Trump hater' after politically charged prayer service
item.description: Trump blasted Bishop Mariann Edgar Budde after she used her prayer service remarks to deliver left-wing remarks directed toward Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Trump voters express reservations with his sweeping Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: One Trump voter told NPR he supported pardons related to the Capitol attack, but has a tougher time reconciling pardons for rioters who were violent with police.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez and Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration strips schools, churches of immigration enforcement protections
item.description: The memo rescinds a Biden administration guideline that created "protected areas" consisting of places where "children gather, disaster or emergency relief sites, and social services establishments.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Democratic AGs sue Trump over birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: Twenty-two Democrat-led states and two cities challenged President Trump’s executive order restricting birthright citizenship, that on Tuesday kicked off the first legal battles between his new Justice Department and Democratic attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Refugees set to resettle in Minnesota next month now barred entry after Trump order
item.description: Jane Graupman, the executive director of International Institute of Minnesota, and Nasreen Sajady, the executive director of the Afghan Cultural Society, joined Minnesota Now to talk about the impacts of Trump’s order to temporarily suspend all refugee resettlement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up fight over Pennsylvania mail ballot date requirement
item.description: The Supreme Court said Tuesday it won’t hear civil rights groups’ appeal seeking to invalidate Pennsylvania's dating requirement for mail ballots, which has invalidated thousands of votes each recent election and become a central legal fight over voting in the key swing state.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
